spec_match.rationale: Models the per-source cap accurately for one of the two modes, but the chart is purely client-side synthetic — no fetch to the backend route — so unlike the sibling concepts the demo never proves the real Attribution-Reporting-Register-Trigger header is emitted differently.

interactive.rationale: Two range sliders (conversions-per-source, sources/day) plus a mode toggle drive a live histogram and an attempted/reported/dropped stat row; no static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises sliders for conversion + source count with a histogram and dropped-event counter; page delivers that with the explicit mode toggle making the pre/post regimes a one-click flip.

edge_cases.rationale: Multi-source case is handled (chart adds one bar pair per source), but no support for mixed mode (some sources with ctx_id, some without), no per-source noise budget visualisation, and no upper-bound feedback when sources × n exceeds practical browser storage.

references.rationale: The see-also section now includes the feature index, sibling concepts, the trigger_context_id spec anchor, and chromestatus.com/feature/5079048977645568.

design_system.rationale: .stat.bad { background: var(--accent-rose); color: white } and .stat.good { background: var(--accent-emerald); color: white } — accent-rose with white text needs a contrast check (rose tends to be a mid-tone red; AA at 1.6rem is 3:1, which is plausible but not verified). The col-toggle uses font-mono buttons that correctly override colour with background.

openQuestions.title: Wire the backend trigger route in
openQuestions.detail: The sibling concepts POST to ../trigger-context-id-builder/register-trigger and surface the response headers; this probe is purely client-side. Either fetch the backend per source-bar (proving the wire format) or add a banner that explicitly says 'simulation only — see per-conversion-stream for the real header path'.

openQuestions.title: Verify .stat.bad / .stat.good contrast
openQuestions.detail: Background --accent-rose with foreground 'white' (literal, not the design token --bg-ivory) at 1.6rem font-display: spot-check the contrast. The 0.72rem .lbl on the same background is borderline and may fail WCAG AA for small text.

openQuestions.title: Show mixed-mode (per-trigger) regime
openQuestions.detail: Real campaigns set trigger_context_id on some triggers and not others. Add a third mode toggle ('mixed: 50% of triggers carry ctx_id') so the histogram shows the partial uplift. That's a likely real-world pattern as advertisers migrate.

summary: Clean histogram and the mode toggle makes the pre/post regimes obvious. The reference links are now present; remaining work is to either wire the backend header route into this probe or label it explicitly as a simulation.
